# UC-19.2.9 · HCI Data Resiliency and Rebuild Progress

## Description

After node or disk failure, rebuild must complete before another failure. Monitoring rebuild progress and ETA ensures data remains protected.

## Value

After node or disk failure, rebuild must complete before another failure. Monitoring rebuild progress and ETA ensures data remains protected.

## Implementation

Poll resiliency and rebuild status. Alert when rebuild is slow or ETA exceeds threshold. Report on rebuild history and time-to-full resilience. Correlate with disk and node events.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Nutanix/vSAN resiliency APIs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Rebuild progress %, ETA, affected containers/vSAN components.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll resiliency and rebuild status. Alert when rebuild is slow or ETA exceeds threshold. Report on rebuild history and time-to-full resilience. Correlate with disk and node events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:resiliency"
| where status="rebuilding" OR status="rebalancing"
| stats latest(progress_pct) as progress, latest(eta_sec) as eta, latest(affected_gb) as gb by cluster, task_type
| table cluster, task_type, progress, eta, gb
```

Understanding this SPL

**HCI Data Resiliency and Rebuild Progress** — After node or disk failure, rebuild must complete before another failure. Monitoring rebuild progress and ETA ensures data remains protected.

Documented **Data sources**: Rebuild progress %, ETA, affected containers/vSAN components. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Nutanix/vSAN resiliency APIs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: nutanix:resiliency. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="nutanix:resiliency". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where status="rebuilding" OR status="rebalancing"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster, task_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **HCI Data Resiliency and Rebuild Progress**): table cluster, task_type, progress, eta, gb


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Nutanix Prism (health, storage, and alerts) for the same cluster and time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (rebuild progress), Table (active rebuilds), Line chart (rebuild rate).

## SPL

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="nutanix:resiliency"
| where status="rebuilding" OR status="rebalancing"
| stats latest(progress_pct) as progress, latest(eta_sec) as eta, latest(affected_gb) as gb by cluster, task_type
| table cluster, task_type, progress, eta, gb
```

## Visualization

Gauge (rebuild progress), Table (active rebuilds), Line chart (rebuild rate).
